title: Consumer substitution after stockout
prompt: Before a selling period, a merchant allocates inventory across two substitute products. If a requested item is unavailable, a fixed fraction of demand switches to the other item. Develop a framing that distinguishes the allocation decision from realized stockouts and does not claim the substitution fraction is causal evidence.

model_specification.mathematical_objective: maximize expected profit net of holding and shortage costs
decision_variables: initial units allocated to each product
model_specification.mechanism: Stockouts redirect a modeled fraction of unmet requests to available substitute inventory.

prohibited_inferences: Observed stockouts are selected by the merchant
